EHR & Virtual Care Ad Placements

Presented by Saba Daniel  ·  May 21, 2026  ·  22 min session
What is an EHR Clinical Workflow Populus Touchpoints Ad Placements Steering Rules Compliance Measurement
1

Healthcare Systems: Canada vs. the US

Saba opened with why EHRs exist and how they differ between countries, because the US fragmentation is central to understanding why Populus's work is complex. Canada operates a single, national electronic health record that spans the full continuum of care. The US started from the same goal with the 2009 HITECH Act ("Meaningful Use") but ended up with hundreds of fragmented EHR vendors, leading to the ongoing push for interoperability through health exchanges, FHIR APIs, and the 2016 CURES Act.

Slide — Healthcare Systems: Canada vs. United States

🍁 Canada

  • Single-payer, universal coverage
  • Provincial systems, nationally coordinated
  • One unified patient record across care
  • Simpler billing — direct to province

🇺🇸 United States

  • Multi-payer — private + Medicare/Medicaid
  • Hundreds of competing EHR vendors
  • Interoperability still a work in progress
  • FHIR APIs now mandated to bridge gaps
2

What Is an EHR — and How It Differs from an EMR

People use EMR and EHR interchangeably. They're not the same. An EMR (Electronic Medical Record) is limited to a single practice. An EHR (Electronic Health Record) is interoperable, shared across providers, and the modern standard. Epic is the dominant EHR for hospital health systems. Flori (Populus's partner) is not an EHR — it's a middleware gateway connected to EHR systems.

Slide — EHR vs. EMR vs. Paper Records
3

Core Components of an EHR

Every EHR is built around six modules. Understanding which one Populus sits in — and which ones we don't yet touch — matters for product strategy.

Slide — Core EHR Components
Patient Demographics
Name, DOB, insurance, contacts — captured at scheduling.
Medical History
Past diagnoses, surgeries, allergies, family history.
Clinical Notes
SOAP notes: Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan.
Medications & Orders
E-prescribing, CPOE.
Where Populus operates today
Lab & Diagnostics
Results, imaging — all visible in the patient portal.
Future opportunity
Clinical Decision Support
Drug interactions, care gaps, protocols.
Future opportunity
Saba's point: Every one of those six modules could be a separate product or two. Right now we have a foothold in Medications & Orders. Lab results, patient portal messaging, and clinical decision support are all areas to grow into.
4

The Clinical Workflow — Where Populus Fits

A patient visit moves through a defined sequence inside the EHR. Populus can only place messaging at specific points, and the steering rules below dictate exactly where that line is.

Scheduling
›
Check-In
›
Triage
›
Provider Encounter
›
Order Entry (CPOE)
›
Lab / Imaging
›
Clinical Decision
›
E-Prescribe
›
Billing
›
Follow-Up
Slide — EHR Clinical Workflow
Physician Steering Rule (Critical): We cannot message a provider about a specific drug before they have chosen that drug. Once the medication has been selected and e-prescribed, brand awareness, co-pay, and affordability messaging is permitted. Doing it before that point is physician steering — it is prohibited.
What IS allowed before e-prescribe: Condition-level messaging to a patient (e.g., "Your A1C suggests you may be at risk for diabetes") is acceptable at the clinical decision step. Recommending a specific drug is not.
5

Key EHR Modules & Populus Touchpoints

Slide — Key EHR Modules & Features

Two modules Saba called out as current and near-term Populus territory:

Patient Portal (Now)

  • Test results, visit summaries, refill requests
  • Populus has active messaging here
  • Deeper product opportunities remain
  • Lab results live here too — post-visit messaging

Pharmacy Networks (Near-Term)

  • Medications flow from e-prescribe to pharmacies
  • Labs flow to external labs
  • Opportunity to message pharmacist or patient
  • Saba's primary new business focus
6

Virtual Care vs. EHR Ad Placements

The second deck covered where ads actually live. Virtual care platforms and hospital EHR systems are entirely different environments with different audiences, dwell times, and compliance expectations.

Slide — Virtual Care vs. EHR Basics

Virtual Care (Our Space)

  • Waiting room — 2-8 min, high attention
  • Pre-consult intake — condition relevance
  • Post-consult summary — action-ready patient
  • Rx confirmation — high-intent moment
  • HCP portal / Rx pad

Hospital EHR (Not Primary)

  • Clinical notes, SOAP documentation
  • Inpatient & surgical workflows
  • Deeply IT-managed infrastructure
  • Much harder to penetrate
Slide — Key Ad Touchpoints in Virtual Care
7

HCP & Patient Audiences

Slide — HCP & Patient Audiences
8

Compliance Essentials

Three compliance layers apply. Saba noted this is actually less complex than it sounds once you know the rules.

Slide — Healthcare Advertising Compliance
Patient Privacy (HIPAA)
  • Use de-identified audience segments
  • Work only on HIPAA-compliant platforms
  • Never use PHI in targeting or retargeting
Ad Claims (FDA / FTC)
  • FDA-approved indication language only
  • Include fair balance for pharma ads
  • Substantiate all efficacy claims
Never Do
  • No "cures" / "eliminates" / "guaranteed"
  • No off-label promotion
  • No PHI-based retargeting
  • No direct patient prescribing suggestions
POCMA: Point of Care Marketing Association. Sets the industry standard for ad placements in clinical settings. Yasmeen has asked Terry to complete POCMA training — 3 of 6 modules done.
9

Measurement That Actually Matters

Clicks and impressions are what agencies ask for. They are not what healthcare outcomes look like. Saba was direct: the most meaningful conversations start with these conversions.

Slide — Measurement That Actually Matters
Vanity Metrics (What agencies default to) Meaningful Conversions (What to push for)
Impressions / CPM Appointment scheduled after virtual visit
Click-through rate (CTR) Prescription filled
Time on page Refill requested — adherence signal
Social shares Patient support program enrolled
Ad engagement rate HCP sample requested — strong intent
Context: Value-based care is changing physician reimbursement. Doctors are no longer paid just for the volume of visits — they're increasingly paid on how well-controlled their patient population is. That makes medication adherence, refill rates, and clinical outcomes central to what providers care about. Our measurement story should align with that.
